author: fnenna
body:
#### PR description:

This PR addresses the integration of ME0 chambers into the muon system and their inclusion in the PF algorithm within the Phase-2 reconstruction framework. The main changes concern the computation of `segmentCompatibility` and `caloCompatibility`.

**segmentCompatibility**

The ME0 station is now taken into account, assigned to index `i == 5`. The compatibility computation follows an approach similar to CSC and DT segments, with one key difference: due to the coarse -partitioning of ME0 modules, `|d|` is replaced by `d(dx/dz)`. Additionally, a reweighting based on the number of hits per segment is applied to reduce the fake rate. As a result, muons with a segment in the ME0 station can now receive a non-zero `segmentCompatibility`.

**caloCompatibility**

The algorithm has been modified to return a neutral value when the muon candidate falls within the HGCAL pseudorapidity coverage (`|| > 1.52`). This is necessary because `caloCompatibility` is computed assuming the Phase-1 geometry and does not account for the new endcap calorimeter. Consequently, `TM2DCompatibility` relies solely on `segmentCompatibility` when running in Phase-2 geometry, since `caloCompatibility` is not reliable in that regime.

**PFMuonAlgorithm**

No changes are applied under Phase-1 conditions. Under Phase-2 conditions:
- for `|| < 2.3`: the same selection cuts as in Run 3 are retained;
- for `|| > 2.3`: the cuts are loosened to require only `nTrackerHits > 8`.

To allow the algorithm to determine whether it is running in Phase-1 or Phase-2 conditions via an external parameter, a dedicated configuration file has been added for the PFMuon algorithm. This configuration passes the Phase-1 parameters alongside a new boolean flag, `has_ME0`.

  - what other PRs or externals it depends upon if any,
  - link to any additional material useful to provide a documentation for this PR (slides, JIRA tickets, related pull requestes, hypernews, TWiki or Indico pages)  -->

#### PR validation:

This PR has been validated using the standard reconstruction scheme.
title: Integration of ME0 station in the PF muon algorithm
